content.text: I'll read each frame and judge against the rubric.

[
  {
    "ruleId": "V1",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The final frames give the meter, its lilac readout '58', and the closing line clear focus while the two 'you'/'the role' columns recede to dimmed supporting context.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V2",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Content accumulates gradually across frames (arc, then meter, then columns row by row, then products and readout), never flooding more than the two paired columns and one dial at once.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V3",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Each value sits on its row ('Python hours: 12', 'SQL reps: 5'), columns are headed by 'you' and 'the role', and 'two arrows in, one number out' labels the 58 readout directly.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V4",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "All text is either a row label, the readout label, or the short closing annotation 'Different aisle, same meter.' sitting beneath the meter it describes.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V5",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Column headers, row entries, the '58' readout and both bottom lines are all comfortably above the ~2.2% legibility threshold.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V6",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "All columns, the meter, readout, and closing line sit well inside the frame with generous margins.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V7",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "No text or labels overlap; the products (36, 20, 2) sit cleanly in the central gutter between the two columns.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V8",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Input values wear periwinkle blue consistently while the products and pooled readout stay lilac throughout, and the dial needle matches the lilac readout.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V9",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Every element—the two attribute columns, the meter, the needle, and the readout—carries propositional content; no decorative filler is present.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V10",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The promised composition lands: 'you'/'the role' columns with Python hours, SQL reps, nights free multiply and pool to a lilac 58 on the dial, labeled 'two arrows in, one number out' with 'Different aisle, same meter.' as the closing line.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V11",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "evidence": "Matched entries and their products align across the central gutter and the readout sits directly under the meter, keeping related items adjacent.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V12",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "evidence": "Across the six frames the meter forms, columns build row by row, and the products pool into the readout as a continuous progression rather than a hard cut.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V13",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "evidence": "The layout has ample negative space with the two columns balanced left and right and the meter centered below, yielding a clear reading order.",
    "suggestion": ""
  }
]
